channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 65, single, seeking a roommate: More seniors are being priced out of living alone
item.description: Roommates overall are skewing older, as young people stay with their parents for longer. The share of older adults looking to rent with a roommate has tripled from a decade ago.
item.content:encoded: Roommates overall are skewing older, as young people stay with their parents for longer. The share of older adults looking to rent with a roommate has tripled from a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Ole Miss hopes to tackle college gambling
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Daniel Durkin, founder of the University of Mississippi's Center for Collegiate Gambling, about gambling among students and the rise of gambling addictions.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Daniel Durkin, founder of the University of Mississippi's Center for Collegiate Gambling, about gambling among students and the rise of gambling addictions.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: More and more people experiencing homelessness have been staying at airports
item.description: As homelessness increases in cities around the country, it's also increasing at airports. Some places are upping outreach efforts at airports to get people connected with shelter or services.
item.content:encoded: As homelessness increases in cities around the country, it's also increasing at airports. Some places are upping outreach efforts at airports to get people connected with shelter or services.
item.dc:creator: Erin Bump

item.title: Trump's ballroom fight sheds new light on an underground White House bunker
item.description: The status of a decades-old bunker beneath the now-demolished East Wing is unclear, but the Trump administration has cited security concerns in its legal filings in favor of continuing construction.
item.content:encoded:
The status of a decades-old bunker beneath the now-demolished East Wing is unclear, but the Trump administration has cited security concerns in its legal filings in favor of continuing construction.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: A U.S. jet goes down over Iran, a U.S. official confirms
item.description: A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. official said that one crew member had been rescued and U.S. forces continue to search for the second crew member.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: Trump budget seeks $1.5 trillion in defense spending alongside domestic program cuts
item.description: In his annual budget, President Trump is asking Congress to boost defense spending to $1.5 trillion, the largest such request in decades.
item.content:encoded:
In his annual budget, President Trump is asking Congress to boost defense spending to $1.5 trillion, the largest such request in decades.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with The Atlantic's Nancy Youssef.
item.content:encoded: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with The Atlantic's Nancy Youssef.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Verdicts against Meta and Google may bring a new era of big tech accountability
item.description: Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.
item.content:encoded:
Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: After the release of the Epstein files, why have there been so few arrests?
item.description: Legal experts tell NPR five possible reasons that, despite the accusations made against rich and powerful people in the files, the DOJ has made no additional arrests. The big one? Lack of evidence.
item.content:encoded:
Legal experts tell NPR five possible reasons that, despite the accusations made against rich and powerful people in the files, the DOJ has made no additional arrests. The big one? Lack of evidence.

(Image credit: Jon Elswick)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit, and 4 astronauts now head to the moon
item.description: With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.
item.content:encoded:
With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce
